[2018-05-07 23:05] amalloy: is it still good value to deny absorb if your opponent is soaking with vigilant attackers? i played np9gs-@PFP2, and there were turns i could click thermite to deny 5 Centurion absorb, or click my Pixies to destroy a Borehole or two but give the opponent full absorb
[2018-05-07 23:15] 1m2o: I think you played it good. You also weren't under a lot of pressure so yeah i don't think it was worth it to click it earlier than you did. Also Frost Broder with absorb denial should be devastating for your opponent.
[2018-05-07 23:18] amalloy: my second animus should probably have been a conduit, since i only actually used 3 of its red. but sure, i'm not really worried about playing it well enough to beat masterbot, so much as learning what to do next time i have a similar choice against a human
[2018-05-07 23:20] 1m2o: I'm probably around your level but yeah i'd say if you're not under pressure just hold it. I mean if you can kill a lot of attackers it's probably right to do it but 2 borehole doesn't seem like a lot
[2018-05-07 23:24] amalloy: i guess the way to think about it is: each turn i'm giving myself 5 damage by clicking the thermites, but also giving him 1 damage by leaving the borehole alive. so i come out ahead on value as long as i'm not letting him kill anything important with that damage
[2018-05-07 23:24] itsotter: 5 damage is more than 1 damage
[2018-05-07 23:24] mtanzer: I'd be willing to pay 4 damage or so to kill a patroller
[2018-05-07 23:25] mtanzer: if you're killing a patroller for 2 damage (its health) I consider that a 2 damage win or so
[2018-05-07 23:25] mtanzer: on t10 for example you'd be killing it for 7 (5 absorb, plus the 2 damage onto the patroller)
[2018-05-07 23:26] mtanzer: and on t11 you have the option to kill 2 patrollers for essentially 9 by the same reasoning
[2018-05-07 23:26] amalloy: 4 is a higher number than i expected. 2 for the HP, 1 for the damage it's doing, and then a bit more for inflation?
[2018-05-07 23:26] mtanzer: I consider an attacker to be worth around 4hp in general
[2018-05-07 23:27] itsotter: there's the fact that patroller can keep firing as the game continues but when you have a bunch of thermites i feel like you're gonna be going full damage in a couple turns at most
[2018-05-07 23:27] itsotter: so i don't really respect it as a permanent attacker i guess although the situation could be different
[2018-05-07 23:28] mtanzer: I don't follow...patroller is a permanent attacker?
[2018-05-07 23:28] itsotter: yes but gain +5 damage this turn by not killing it
[2018-05-07 23:28] itsotter: click all your damage next turn
[2018-05-07 23:28] itsotter: what have you lost in exchange for that +5 damage
[2018-05-07 23:29] mtanzer: I guess I didn't say it explicitly but I also thought clicking thermites was correct
[2018-05-07 23:29] itsotter: he's not magically going to be able to defend without losing it next turn
[2018-05-07 23:29] mtanzer: oh you think he can't defend it
[2018-05-07 23:29] mtanzer: umm there was aegis in the set and the bot had some floated green
[2018-05-07 23:30] amalloy: i think @itsotter  is saying, if we have the option to kill borehole now, by clicking thermite we will probably have the opportunity to kill it next turn too
[2018-05-07 23:30] amalloy: not a guarantee, but plausible
[2018-05-07 23:31] itsotter: or if we don't, he has to spend his whole turn aegis and forcefielding or something to add like +9 defense to what he had previously? and we keep addiing damage and are happy
[2018-05-07 23:31] mtanzer: well we're happy regardless because we were winning before these decisions occurred
[2018-05-07 23:31] itsotter: he needs +2 to cover the borehole, +5 to cover the thermite damage you just gained, probably another +2 for the attackers you're adding
[2018-05-07 23:31] itsotter: a ridiculous amount of extra defense
[2018-05-07 23:32] mtanzer: I still think 4 damage to kill an attacker being fair is a nice rule of thumb.  maybe slightly less.  that's how I think about decisions like that anyway
[2018-05-07 23:33] mtanzer: I get that number from doing some inflation math but you could probably also reach it by comparing the price of attackers and soak
[2018-05-07 23:33] amalloy: @mtanzer isn't it usually better to snipe wall than tarsier, with apollo?
[2018-05-07 23:34] amalloy: the defensive tempo is worth a lot
[2018-05-07 23:34] mtanzer: no you definitely snipe tarsier
[2018-05-07 23:34] mtanzer: in general.  I mean I'm sure there's exceptions

[2018-05-08 08:30] mtanzer: I think people underrate buying nonprompt walls.  compared to buying 3 engis you're saving a gold.
reasons you might not to buy the W:
-you need granularity
-there's something better you can buy this turn instead
-you might need less than 3 additional soak next turn
-maybe you're overteched and a drone or two would help

reasons to buy are pretty simple, you'd buy it in many of the same spots you'd buy some engineers, when you have leftover gold and soak the following turn is useful.
